name: Gold Standard Default Army
engine_principle: Build the strongest skill engine first. Do not place dragons only to distribute Sentinels across armies.
why_it_is_default: Vhagar remains the vanguard because it is the center tank and control piece.
Rhysarion supplies early-round damage suppression through Ebbing Fury and later Recovery timing.
Malachite supplies team amplification and Tactical mitigation through Forest's Instinct while adding meaningful damage through Warden's Rally.
The trio combines tanking, early damage suppression, team support, recovery timing, damage output, and full troop affinity.
Recent Dock battle testing showed this engine trading slightly ahead even while down troops.

vanguard.reason: Keep Vhagar center unless battle reports prove it cannot survive. It absorbs center pressure and keeps Fiery Bonds active.

flanks.reason: This protects Rhysarion from specific right-lane pressure seen in the Dock test while keeping Malachite available as a durable flank support/damage piece.

rules_for_future_armies: Do not build armies by Sentinel quota.
Do not break 3/3 troop affinity for theorycraft unless battle reports show the skill synergy wins anyway.
Every main army should be judged against this gold-standard engine: tank, stabilizer, support/debuff, finishing damage, and full troop affinity.
Flank swaps are allowed for specific enemy lane pressure, but Vhagar remains the default vanguard.
notes: Older repo files may list Malachite left and Rhysarion right. This file records the updated default framing requested on June 27, 2026: Rhysarion / Vhagar / Malachite as the gold-standard engine.
If later battle reports show Malachite-left performs better generally, this default should be revised with evidence.
